text:
🔭 Multi-agent orchestration: supervisor vs P2P vs scripted workflows

TL;DR: Supervisor and scripted workflows are the two patterns Alex actually designs. P2P is a platform prerogative in Claude Code — dynamic workflows auto-generate adversarial cross-checking topology; you don't wire agents together. Hand-designed P2P only makes sense with an external framework (LangGraph, Swarms.world) for continuously running distributed systems where a supervisor is a real SPOF — not for bounded research/content tasks.

Key points:
· Claude Code has 4 primitives: skill → subagent → agent team (supervisor) → workflow (scripted). Each maps to a scale tier. [Fact / Anthropic official docs]
· Workflows: Claude writes a JS script; runtime executes; intermediate results stay in script variables (not context). Trigger: say "ultracode" or "workflow" in prompt. Available on ALL paid plans (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise). [Fact / Anthropic docs]
· Agent teams = supervisor in Claude Code: lead agent coordinates peer sessions via shared task list. Default enterprise pattern. [Fact / Anthropic docs + practitioner consensus]
· P2P in Claude Code = platform-handled: workflows spawn adversarial sub-agents automatically. Hand-designing P2P only when building on LangGraph/Swarms for production distributed infrastructure. [Inference + practitioner consensus]
